legal.disclaimer: Grades are algorithmic assessments for reference only, not veterinary or regulatory evaluations. Consult your veterinarian before making dietary changes.
legal.natureOfServiceBody: ToxicPaw provides algorithmic ingredient analysis for educational and informational purposes only. Grades and ratings are generated by automated algorithms and do not constitute veterinary advice, regulatory assessments, or product safety certifications.
legal.notVetAdviceBody: Information provided should not replace professional veterinary consultation. Always consult a licensed veterinarian before making changes to your pet’s diet.
legal.analysisLimitationsBody: Our ingredient database reflects general nutritional perspectives. Individual ingredients may be FDA/AAFCO approved and safe at regulated levels. Our flagging system reflects algorithmic categorization, not definitive safety determinations. Factors such as manufacturing quality, bioavailability, and individual pet health conditions are not evaluated.
legal.brandRatingsBody: Brand grades are based solely on ingredient composition analysis. They do not evaluate manufacturing standards, quality control, recall history, or regulatory compliance. A low grade does not indicate that a product is unsafe or unsuitable for your pet. Many products with lower algorithmic grades are recommended by veterinarians for specific health conditions.
legal.noLiabilityBody: ToxicPaw and its creators assume no liability for decisions made based on information provided by this service.
legal.disputeProcessBody: If you represent a brand and believe our analysis contains errors, please open an issue on our GitHub repository at https://github.com/toffee-desuwa/toxicpaw/issues. For a detailed explanation of how our scoring algorithm works, see our Methodology page. We also welcome feedback from veterinary professionals and researchers via GitHub Issues.

methodology.overviewBody: ToxicPaw uses an algorithmic scoring system to analyze pet food ingredient lists. Our goal is to provide pet owners with an accessible, transparent tool for understanding ingredient composition. This page documents exactly how our scoring works so that our assessments can be independently evaluated.
methodology.dataSourcesBody:
Our ingredient database of 500+ entries is informed by the following sources:

• AAFCO (Association of American Feed Control Officials) ingredient definitions and guidelines
• FDA Center for Veterinary Medicine pet food safety guidance
• Published veterinary nutrition literature
• Publicly available ingredient safety data

Each ingredient in our database is assigned a safety rating (safe, caution, or harmful) and a category (e.g., protein, grain, filler, byproduct, coloring, sweetener). These classifications reflect general nutritional perspectives and are not regulatory determinations.
methodology.howScoringWorksBody:
When you scan or search a pet food, our system:

1. Parses the ingredient list and normalizes each ingredient name
2. Looks up each ingredient in our database using exact match, alias match, or fuzzy match
3. Assigns each ingredient a flag: green (safe), yellow (caution), red (harmful), or gray (unknown/not in database)
4. Calculates a numeric score from 0 to 100
5. Maps the score to a letter grade (A through F)
6. Generates a plain-language verdict
methodology.ingredientCategoriesBody:
Each ingredient in our database belongs to one of 17 categories: protein, grain, vegetable, fruit, fat/oil, fiber, vitamin, mineral, preservative, additive, sweetener, coloring, filler, byproduct, supplement, thickener, and flavor.

Some categories are treated as "concerning" regardless of their individual safety rating. These include: fillers, byproducts, colorings, and sweeteners. Ingredients in these categories receive an additional score deduction even if they are individually rated as safe, because their presence may indicate lower overall formulation quality.
methodology.scoringRulesBody:
The score starts at 100 and is adjusted as follows:

• Each harmful (red) ingredient: −15 points
• Each harmful ingredient in the top 5 positions: additional −5 points
• Each caution (yellow) ingredient: −5 points
• Each caution ingredient in the top 5 positions: additional −3 points
• Each ingredient in a concerning category (filler, byproduct, coloring, sweetener): −3 points
• Bonus: +5 points if the first ingredient is a quality protein source
• Penalty: −10 points if no protein source appears in the top 3 ingredients

Ingredient position matters because pet food regulations require ingredients to be listed in descending order by weight. The first ingredients make up the largest portion of the food.

The final score is clamped to the range 0–100.
methodology.gradeThresholdsBody:
The numeric score maps to a letter grade:

• A: 90–100 (Excellent)
• B: 75–89 (Good)
• C: 60–74 (Average)
• D: 40–59 (Below Average)
• F: 0–39 (Poor)

These thresholds are fixed in our algorithm and applied consistently to all products.
methodology.petProfileBody: When a user provides their pet's breed, age, weight, or health conditions, the system applies additional score adjustments. For example, a food containing chicken may receive additional deductions for a pet with a known chicken allergy. These adjustments are applied on top of the base score and the result is still clamped to 0–100.
methodology.whatWeDoNotEvaluateBody:
Our analysis is limited to ingredient composition. We do not evaluate:

• Manufacturing quality and processes
• Bioavailability and digestibility
• Batch-to-batch consistency
• Regulatory compliance history
• Individual pet health conditions (unless a pet profile is provided)
• Breed-specific nutritional needs (unless a pet profile is provided)
• Caloric density or guaranteed analysis values
• Recall history
methodology.limitationsBody:
Please consider the following when interpreting results:

• Our database may not include every ingredient. Unrecognized ingredients are marked as "unknown" and do not affect the score positively or negatively.
• Ingredient safety is context-dependent. Dose, species, and individual health all matter.
• Many flagged ingredients are FDA/AAFCO approved and safe at regulated levels. A flag reflects algorithmic categorization, not a definitive safety determination.
• This tool supplements but does not replace veterinary guidance.
• OCR-based scanning may introduce errors in ingredient text recognition.
methodology.openToFeedbackBody: We strive for accuracy and transparency. If you are a brand representative, veterinary professional, or researcher and believe our analysis contains errors or could be improved, we welcome your feedback. Submit feedback via our GitHub repository at https://github.com/toffee-desuwa/toxicpaw/issues. We are committed to updating our database based on credible evidence.
